title: librush
description: A library mod adding support for a couple of new features related to archeology and brushes, like ability to make brushing faster.
body:
A library mod that expands what is possible in Minecraft related to brushes and archeology.

Fabric version requires Fabric API. Fabric version should work with Quilt as well as long as you're using Fabric API or (assuming it's available) Quilted Fabric API / Quilt Standard Libraries.

Primarily to be used in combination with other mods that require this one. However, there's a few minor features that may be used without any additional mods.

Current features:

*   A new attribute that determines the duration of a single sweep with a brush, allowing for slower/faster brushing.
*   (NeoForge only for now) configurable base duration of a single sweep with a brush.
*   (Fabric only, not needed for NeoForge) allow brushing armadillos with any modded brushes

Planned features for 1.0:

*   Fabric configuration support.
*   Ability to modify the amount of required sweeps using attributes, similarly to the sweep duration.
*   Example code (using GitHub wiki?) explaining how to use this library, as well as how to keep compatibility with it.

Possible features:

*   Ability to modify the base sweep duration/amount of sweeps required based on the block itself.
*   Ability to define which brushes work on which blocks, similarly to vanilla tool tiers.
*   Ability to modify which brushes work on which animals. Armadillo in vanilla only, would require support from other mods if implemented.
*   Perhaps a backport to older Minecraft versions, if there's interest for it?
*   Implement in-game tests?

 

Do you like my work?
